edges.desc: The Forge of Spells is located within Wave Echo Cave
Human wizards from Phandalin fought alongside dwarves and gnomes to defend Wave Echo Cave
Gundren Rockseeker travels with Sildar Hallwinter as his escort on the road to Phandalin
The Black Spider has Gundren Rockseeker captured and his map taken to Cragmaw Castle
Sildar Hallwinter tells the players about the lost mine and that Gundren was taken to Cragmaw Castle
The players are motivated to rescue their dwarf friend Gundren Rockseeker from captivity
The Black Spider wants the adventurers out of the picture and is their final adversary in Wave Echo Cave
The adventurers confront and fight Nezznar the Black Spider in Wave Echo Cave to end his troublemaking
Players can invent their own reasons for visiting Phandalin or use provided adventure hooks
hires the players in Neverwinter to escort a wagon to Phandalin
has gone ahead to attend to business in the town
travels with Gundren Rockseeker to Phandalin
are in the city of Neverwinter at the start of the quest
will pay the players 10 gp each when they deliver the wagon safely to the trading post
Knights dare the crypts of the fallen dwarf kings of Delzoun, seeking glory and treasure
Wizards plunder the ruins of the fallen Netherese empire, delving into secrets too dark for the light of day
Daring souls delve into the wreckage of ancient strongholds and explore ruins of long-lost cultures in the Sword Coast region
Players are escorting Gundren Rockseeker's wagon of supplies as hired help
Players travel south along the High Road from Neverwinter
Players head east along the Triboar Trail toward Phandalin
Players are a half-day's march from Phandalin in the wilderness
Four goblins are hiding in the woods at Ambush Bend, waiting to ambush travelers
Goblins ambush the players when they approach the dead horses, with two making melee attacks and two making ranged attacks
The goblins use the goblin trail and the last surviving goblin attempts to flee along it when three are defeated
Goblins ambushed and killed Gundren Rockseeker's horses, indicating they attacked his party
Goblins ambushed and killed Sildar Hallwinter's horses, indicating they attacked his party
The players come around a bend and spot dead horses blocking the path
The party pursues the goblins down the trail after defeating the ambushers
The lead character may trigger the hidden snare trap and become restrained upside down
The lead character may fall into the hidden pit trap and take bludgeoning damage
The party arrives at the hideout after following the trail, completing a story milestone
Gundren Rockseeker is traveling with the players according to the Cragmaw tribe's orders
The players have arrived at the cave mouth after following the goblins' trail
Two goblins are stationed at this guard post, though they are bored and inattentive
The goblins serve as lookouts at this guard post and will attack intruders they notice
If the goblins notice intruders, they open fire with their bows, potentially catching characters by surprise
Three wolves are confined in the kennel, kept and trained by the Cragmaws
Wolves attack any creature except goblins that moves into the room, but cannot reach targets on the steps
Characters can attempt to calm the wolves with a DC 15 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check, or DC 10 if given food
Characters remain in sight of the wolves in the kennel, potentially goading them
The players are currently in the steep passage, observing their surroundings
The players are observing the rickety bridge (overpass) from their position in the passage
stands watch on the bridge as a lazy and inattentive guard
Six goblins inhabit this den, using it as a common room and barracks
Held prisoner in this chamber, securely bound on the southern ledge of the cavern
Yeemik (goblin leader) tries to convince the players to kill Klarg, promising to release Sildar in exchange for the bugbear's head
Warns the characters not to trust the goblin's deal, as Yeemik will try to force them to pay a ransom even after completing their part
Sildar met Gundren in Neverwinter and agreed to accompany him back to Phandalin to help reopen the old mine
Sildar provides the characters with four pieces of useful information about the Rockseeker brothers, Klarg, the Black Spider, and Iarno Albrek
is present in the goblin den and can be rescued there
Gundren Rockseeker's gear was taken to Cragmaw Castle, which is part of the goblin den location context
Three goblins guard this cave, ready for trouble if warned by the goblin in area 5
Goblins work together - one will flee to area 8 to warn Klarg if a fight breaks out
Klarg the bugbear shares this cave with his pet wolf and two goblins
Klarg's mangy pet wolf Ripper lives in the cave
Two goblins live in the cave under Klarg's command
Klarg the bugbear bullies and commands the goblins under him
The goblins resent Klarg's bullying and leadership
The central fire pit in klargs_cave deals fire damage to any creature that enters or falls prone there
Players are present in Klarg's cave and can interact with the environment
The players explore the Cragmaw hideout as part of their adventure
The players explore Klarg's cave as part of their adventure
The players defeat Klarg and his allies in the Cragmaw hideout, completing a story milestone
The characters first arrive in Phandalin and can visit various locations in the town
The players catch their first glimpse of Phandalin and approach the town
The players go by along the muddy main street as they approach the town
The main street climbs toward the ruined manor house on the hillside
Sildar suggests securing lodgings at the local inn for the party
NPCs at the inn direct characters toward adventure opportunities and important rumors found in other parts of town
takes one of the six rooms for rent at the inn
The townmaster is looking for someone (players) to run off orc raiders on the east end of Triboar Trail
Characters can purchase adventuring gear (except weapons/armor and items over 25 gp) at Barthen's Provisions
Barthen pays the characters for delivering supplies and encourages them to rescue Gundren Rockseeker
Barthen considers Gundren a friend and was excited about his talk of discovering the lost mine
The characters are at Daran Edermath's cottage beside the apple orchard
Old Owl Well is described as ruins that are an old watchtower of the ancient Netherese empire
Linene Graywind warns the characters about the Redbrands and advises them to avoid the Sleeping Giant tap house
Halia Thornton suggests the goblin minion might know Cragmaw Castle's location and leverages this to persuade the characters to help her deal with the Redbrands
Halia tells the players that Redbrands loiter around the Sleeping Giant tap house and offers them 100 gp to eliminate Glasstaff
Halia tells the players that Redbrands have a base under Tresendar Manor on the east edge of town
Halia offers the players 100 gp to eliminate Glasstaff and bring her any correspondence found in the leader's quarters
The Redbrands have their base under Tresendar Manor on the east edge of town
Glasstaff's quarters are located within the Redbrand hideout under Tresendar Manor
The party is at the Miners' Exchange location where these events occur
Qelline Alderleaf is willing to let the characters stay in her hayloft at the farm
Carp informs the characters that the secret tunnel leads to area 8 in the Redbrand hideout
Qelline suggests the characters visit Reidoth at the ruins of Thundertree to get help finding specific sites
Sister Garaele offers the quest to the characters to bring Agatha a jeweled silver comb and persuade her to reveal the location of Bowgentle's spellbook
The party is at the Shrine of Luck where events are occurring
The characters can choose to visit this rundown tap house at the end of Phandalin's main street
The notice posted on the board offers a reward for dealing with orcs near Wyvern Tor, inviting those interested to inquire within
establishes himself at the townmaster's hall after resting at Stonehill Inn
as an agent of the Lords' Alliance, his goal is to bring law and order to Phandalin
suggests the party might find Cragmaw Castle by searching the lands around the Triboar Trail for more raiding parties
suggests searching the wilderness around Phandalin for Cragmaw goblin raiding parties to locate their castle
the cellars of Tresendar Manor have been converted into a Redbrand stronghold
if the characters investigate this place, they find the entrance to the Redbrand hideout
decide to confront the Redbrands at the tap house after speaking with NPCs
decide to investigate the manor, skipping the encounter
go straight to the hideout after investigating Tresendar Manor
can be confronted by players at the Sleeping Giant tap house
can confront players in the street
attacks players after insults and baiting if players don't attack first
last remaining ruffian flees toward Tresendar Manor when three are defeated
The players approach the Sleeping Giant tap house and are being watched by the ruffians on the porch
The players are heading back into the street where they encounter armed ruffians
Townmaster Harbin Wester warns players not to cause trouble after they kill Redbrands, fearing retaliation
The cellars served as safe storage for the manor before it was ruined
An adjoining crypt provided a resting place for deceased Tresendar family members
The Redbrands added slave pens when expanding the cellars
The Redbrands added barracks when expanding the cellars
The Redbrands added workshops when expanding the cellars
If the characters begin their search at Tresendar Manor, they enter the dungeon
can be used to pick the locked door in the hideout with a successful Dexterity check
The characters open the door and enter the cellar to explore it
The characters' noisy searching attracts the attention of the Redbrands in area 2
contained in a waterproof satchel hidden in the cistern as part of Iarno's getaway kit
contained in a waterproof satchel hidden in the cistern as part of Iarno's getaway kit
Three Redbrand ruffians are resting in this barracks room
The players are present in the barracks where the Redbrands' belongings are found
Characters can search the hall for traps and attempt to navigate around the pit trap
Three skeletons are animated and present in the Tresendar crypts
The skeletons attack any creature that comes within 10 feet of the doors, unless wearing a Redbrand cloak or speaking the password 'Illefarn'
The skeletons guard the doors leading to areas 5 and 6 within the crypts
Fighting in this room alerts the Redbrands in 5 that trouble is on the way
Two Redbrand ruffians stand guard in the slave pens, taunting prisoners and preparing to ambush intruders
The cell doors in the slave pens feature simple locks that require thieves' tools and a successful Dexterity check to pick
The Dendrar family is grateful to the characters for rescuing them from the slave pens
The secret door from the armory leads to location 7, which players can discover
The players are present in the armory chamber
Players are exploring the storeroom work area where they find various goods
The nothic lurks near the west ends of the two bridges in the crevasse cavern, having been lured there by a faint magical effect emanating from the crevasse
The nothic serves as guardian of the cave, watching for intruders and hiding behind stone columns to observe them
The nothic attempts to use its Weird Insight to discern the characters' secrets when it notices intruders entering the cave
Players are exploring the crevasse cavern where the bridges are located
The chest containing the hoard is hidden in a cubbyhole at the bottom of the crevasse, under the north bridge, and can be found by characters who descend into the fissure.
three bugbears are present in the guard barracks
one goblin named Droop is present in the guard barracks
bugbears will attack characters unless they are wearing scarlet cloaks or successfully deceive them
players can persuade bugbears to help deal with 'traitors' or 'impostors' with a successful Charisma (Deception) check
The players have suddenly appeared in the guard barracks, causing the goblin to faint
The party is fighting bugbears in the guard barracks
Four Redbrand ruffians are drinking and playing knucklebones in the common room, which serves as their headquarters and meeting room
The knucklebones game is close to turning acrimonious as most of their games do, with one ruffian using loaded dice to win
If characters burst into the room, they automatically surprise the Redbrand ruffians and can attack them
The players are present in the common room observing the scene
The party can defeat the Redbrands in this room to earn 400 XP divided equally among characters
mentioned in Urmon's journal as being lost in Wave Echo Cave, which is described in the workshop's tome
The players are present in the wizard's workshop, observing the room
Iarno's apparatus is set up to brew this potion in his workshop, though he hasn't succeeded yet
Iarno grabs his staff of defense from his quarters when warned of approaching trouble
Iarno 'Glasstaff' Albrek is the leader of the Redbrands and resides in these quarters
Iarno can be surprised and fought by the players if they approach through the secret passage
Iarno quaffs the potion of invisibility from his satchel when fleeing from pursuing characters
Iarno uses his staff of defense in his quarters for protection and spellcasting
Players confront Iarno in his quarters, leading to combat where he uses his staff of defense
The characters search Iarno's quarters and find various papers and a letter
Arranges to have Iarno incarcerated in the townmaster's hall until transport to Neverwinter
Arranges for Iarno to be transported back to Neverwinter for potential trial
strangers are due to arrive in Phandalin according to spies in Neverwinter
Lord Albrek instructs Iarno to capture or kill the strangers if they upset their plans
Lord Albrek has spies in Neverwinter who are monitoring the players' movements
is being held at Cragmaw Castle according to Sildar Hallwinter's quest
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during day or night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during day or night
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during the day
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during the day
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during day or night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during day or night
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during day or night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during day or night
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during day or night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during day or night
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during day or night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during day or night
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during day or night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during day or night
can be encountered along the Triboar Trail during the night
can be encountered in the wilderness around Phandalin during the night
The adventurers are traveling through the Sword Coast wilderness
The players are present at Owlbear Tower where they are being pursued
The characters enter Agatha's lair to speak with the banshee
If the characters exercise caution and remember what they've come for, they will be able to speak with the banshee
If the characters are rude, disrespectful, or threatening, Agatha scowls and disappears
The players follow the trail and discover the dome-like shelter with a low doorway leading inside
The players are present in Agatha's Lair, experiencing the cold air and feeling of dread
Hamun Kost controls the zombies as undead guardians and can call them off during combat
Twelve zombies lurk inside the crumbled watchtower and shamble out to guard the site when characters approach
Hamun Kost has set up a campsite at Old Owl Well and is exploring the ruins for arcane lore
Kost offers to provide information if players remove orcs at Wyvern Tor or ask Agatha the banshee about the tower's builder
The players have arrived at the ruins of Old Owl Well and are exploring the location
found in a leather bag inside a chest in Hamun Kost's tent
found in a bone tube inside a leather bag in a chest in Hamun Kost's tent
found in a tiny jeweled box inside a leather bag in a chest in Hamun Kost's tent
gave the party a quest to investigate the Red Wizard's presence at Old Owl Well
is present at Old Owl Well as the Red Wizard
controls zombies that serve him
can defeat Hamun Kost and his zombies for 800 XP
can parley with Hamun Kost for 200 XP
reports back to Daran Edermath in Phandalin to complete the quest
The players are exploring the ruins, following the trail that becomes an old lane winding between dilapidated buildings
Two twig blights hide among the weeds flanking the cottage's open doorway
The blights will attack in self-defense or come to aid other twig blights if combat erupts
The party is present at the westernmost cottage where they can destroy twig blights
six twig blights are lurking among the ordinary foliage in the overgrown cottages
one round after the initial attack, additional twig blights join the fray
Players are searching the interior of the eastern cottage at this location
party destroys twig blights at the blighted cottages
Four ash zombies lurk in the shadows of the former tavern, slumped against walls or under the bar
When living creatures enter, the zombies groan and stir, then pursue any characters they see, attacking until destroyed
The party is at the brown horse location where they can destroy ash zombies
Reidoth offers to guide the party to Wave Echo Cave in exchange for chasing off the dragon in area 7
If the characters attack Reidoth, he transforms into a squirrel and escapes through a crack in the wall
chases off Venomfang from the location
visits Reidoth's cottage to interact with the druid
crawling in the thicket east of the ruined farmhouse
party destroys the twig blights at the blighted farmhouse
two giant spiders hide on the inner walls of the ruined store
spiders attack characters who trigger their webs, surprising those with passive Perception less than 17
spiders use trailing web lines to sense prey moving through webs in the lane
Players are exploring the ruined store where webs fill two squares north of the doorway and square marked '6'
worn by the shriveled elf corpse in the western half of the building
found in a belt pouch on the shriveled elf corpse in the western half
has recently claimed the tower as its lair after searching Neverwinter Wood
Venomfang is a young green dragon
tore its way through the roof to kill the giant spider residents
does not want to give up such a promising lair
Players notice the eerie quiet and strange acrid smell at the location
found in a broken wooden chest on the tower floor containing the dead wizard's treasure
found in a broken wooden chest on the tower floor containing the dead wizard's treasure
two ash zombies are slumped on the floor of the abandoned smithy
The party is at the old smithy location where they can destroy ash zombies
can find a small wooden case hidden in a compartment beneath the storage shelves with a successful Wisdom (Perception) check
The party is at the herbalist's shop where they can return the necklace to Mirna
Five ash zombies lurk within the old garrison building, former members of the garrison who still wear remnants of rusted mail and soldiers' surcoats
The party is present at the old garrison location
six twig blights lurk in the thicket south of the ruined cottage
The party is at the weaver's cottage where they can destroy twig blights
Six cultists are hiding in a cottage in Thundertree, with four standing guard and two resting
Four cultists stand guard while two others rest, indicating a command structure
Four cultists stand guard in the cottage, two in each room
The characters are at the cultist farmhouse location
The orc camp is located at Wyvern Tor, where the orcs and their ogre ally currently lair
A band of bold adventurers dealt with the wyverns that formerly inhabited Wyvern Tor years ago
lone orc sentry guards the camp entrance
six ordinary orcs inhabit the cave in the camp
filthy ogre named Gog inhabits the cave in the camp
Brughor Axe-Biter (orc with 30 hit points) commands the other orcs
Gog the ogre fights alongside the orcs in the cave
characters can attack the lone orc sentry to prevent warning others
party attacks the orc camp at Wyvern Tor
The players ascend a rugged ridge and observe the orc camp from fifty yards away, noticing the cave mouth and the single orc keeping watch
Players are exploring Brughor's cave where they can find treasure
characters might storm Cragmaw Castle with weapons in hand
characters might try to talk their way inside Cragmaw Castle
adventurers are attacked by goblins and hobgoblins from both directions trying to drive them out of the castle
Two goblins occupy each of the two rooms at the archer post
Goblins serve as sentries to drive off intruders who get too close to Cragmaw Castle
Three goblins bunk here in the ruined barracks, making use of every bit of available space
The party is present at the ruined barracks location where they can defeat goblins
Characters can explore the storeroom and interact with its contents
Sildar Hallwinter is present in the storeroom where his equipment is located
Four hobgoblins are quartered in this room
They are quick to defend their tower if any intruders appear
Players are exploring the hobgoblin barracks where weapons are mounted on the walls
The party can defeat the hobgoblins in this room to earn 400 XP divided equally among characters
Seven miserable goblins and their leader Yegg inhabit this hall, which has become a foul goblin mess hall
The players are exploring the banquet hall, observing its current state
The grick lives in this chamber as the special pet of goblin Lhupo
The grick likes to climb up to a ledge hidden in the shadows of the statuary in the higher reaches of the room
The grick quietly observes intruders that enter the area before dropping down to strike, potentially surprising them
Players are exploring the dark hall where the statuette is hidden
Lhupo and two ordinary goblins live in the shrine, wearing filthy robes over armor
Two ordinary goblins serve as acolytes to Lhupo
All three goblins kneel before the altar praying to Maglubiyet, their evil god
The party defeats the goblins at the goblin shrine, earning 150 XP to be divided equally among the characters
can be used to open the locked iron door at the postern gate with a successful Dexterity check
Characters are present near the arrow slits at the postern gate, listening for sounds
can spot a hidden canvas entrance to the ruined tower with a successful Wisdom (Perception) check
The players are exploring the ruined tower, observing its current state and features
Two hobgoblins stand guard in this room, which was once a parlor for the castle's human occupants
The hobgoblins keep watch from the arrow slit and will attack characters creeping around the east side of the castle
captured and confined to this tower by the Cragmaws, kept in the dark room to remain calm
if players don't throw fresh meat, the owlbear attacks the first creature it sees in the doorway
if players throw fresh meat to the owlbear, it devours the food instead of attacking
contained within a battered wooden chest on the jagged ledge of the tower's second floor
contained within a battered wooden chest on the jagged ledge of the tower's second floor
contained within a battered wooden chest on the jagged ledge of the tower's second floor
requires a successful DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) check to notice the chest containing the items from the floor below
party can defeat the owlbear at the tower
party can release the owlbear at the tower
King Grol, the fierce old bugbear, rules from his quarters
Snarl the wolf attends and serves King Grol
Vyerith the doppelganger is negotiating with Grol over the map and Gundren, with conflicting interests
Vyerith intends to question then kill Gundren Rockseeker
Grol holds Gundren hostage and is ready to kill him if threatened
If warned of attack, Vyerith hides behind the northeast door in the king's quarters
The characters may attack King Grol in his quarters
The characters may attack Vyerith the doppelganger in the king's quarters
is located near the southwest corner of the room
is described as a dwarf commoner
three potions of healing are hidden under Grol's bed mattress in a stitched leather sack
hobgoblin war band returning home to Cragmaw Castle
two wolves serve as pets to Targor Bloodsword
Targor Bloodsword leads three hobgoblins in the war band
characters can attempt to persuade Targor to see himself as Grol's successor rather than avenger
players must seek out Wave Echo Cave to discover who the Black Spider is and why he's interested in the Lost Mine of Phandelver
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
can be encountered as wandering monsters throughout the cave
wandering monsters that can attack the party to keep them on their toes
wandering monsters that can attack the party to keep them on their toes
wandering monsters that can attack the party to keep them on their toes
wandering monsters that can attack the party to keep them on their toes
The rhythmic thunder of pounding waves echoes throughout the mine, piquing players' curiosity and luring them toward its source, drawing them deeper into the mine
The characters approach the hidden tunnel entrance in the Sword Mountain foothills
Players are present at the pit trap site and can attempt to climb the walls or fall into the pit
Nezznar overlooked the boots in his haste to explore the rest of Wave Echo Cave
patiently lurking in one of the dead-end passages of the old mine section
nine skeletons form and fight in the guardroom when living creatures enter
The players are exploring this chamber that was once an office or storeroom
required to open the locked iron strongbox behind the counter
Three ghouls are in the south barracks, cracking and gnawing on ancient bones of the fallen
The storeroom is described as a secure resting place for the players/party
The fungal cavern's poisonous gas attacks any creature that attempts to cross it, requiring Constitution saving throws and dealing poison damage to those who fail
Seven ghouls lurk in the shadows on the western ledge of the great cavern
Characters can explore the pool and wade through the stream to 18
clutched in the bony fingers of a skeleton at the dark pool location
Five bugbears reside in this chamber, which was formerly a barracks for miners
Bugbears are here to prevent ghouls, zombies, or other undead from troubling their master in his lair
Eight zombie dwarf warriors rise and pursue living creatures that enter the room
A far more intelligent undead that guards this area to prevent intruders from passing through
This creature was a servant of the human wizards and continues to act on ancient instructions in this chamber
Acts on ancient instructions to prevent intruders (including players) from passing through the area
Characters can sense a subtle aura of magic in the cavern, which becomes stronger as they approach the northern building
spends his time here because his treasure is in the scorched chest
rises up out of the floor and attacks when a living creature enters the room
summoned to guard the magic items created and stored in the Forge of Spells
attacks the party if they attempt to remove anything from the area
can trick the spectator into thinking they are wizards or miners sent to terminate its employment
The players are present in the forge of spells, being addressed by the floating creature
can be created by bathing nonmagical weapons in the brazier's green flame for at least 1 minute
can be created by bathing nonmagical armor in the brazier's green flame for at least 1 minute
located on the worktable in the southeast corner of the forge of spells
located on the worktable in the southeast corner of the forge of spells
The players are currently on a narrow ledge overlooking the booming cavern
Players can use the old streambed as a passage to circumvent the undead in area 12
Three bugbears are stationed in the collapsed cavern
A doppelganger named Vhalak supervises operations in the cavern
Vhalak supervises the bugbears' operation in the cavern
Bugbears guard against undead incursions in the cavern
One bugbear stands guard in the western half of the cavern
Players are exploring the collapsed cavern and must navigate the rift
buried under heavy rubble at the bottom of the rift with a crushed dwarf skeleton, requiring a successful Wisdom (Perception) check to find
uses this room as his headquarters while exploring the mines and searching for the Forge of Spells
four giant spiders defend their master to the death
spiders hide behind pillars and try to entangle intruders in webs before closing to melee range
assumes the guise of Nundro Rockseeker so Nezznar can use the 'dwarf' as leverage to force the party's surrender
retreated to this area and works with Nezznar to deceive the party
have set up a campsite with bedrolls, packs, and a fire pit in front of the statue
The temple contains a statue of Dumathoin with fake emerald eyes protected by abjuration magic, presenting an investigation challenge to characters
Players can deliver captured Nezznar to the townmaster's hall in Phandalin where he is locked up
The townmaster's hall is located in Phandalin where Nezznar would be imprisoned
carried by Nezznar in the temple
carried by Nezznar in the temple
required to open the locked door to the priests' quarters along with a successful Dexterity check
lying on the floor as a prisoner in the room that Nezznar has appropriated as a cell
is described as a dwarf commoner, the youngest of the three Rockseeker brothers
serves as a safe and comfortable place for the players to rest after dealing with Nezznar and his minions
reclaimed the lost mine of Wave Echo Cave from the Black Spider
cleared Phandalin of the ruffians who threatened its people
takes over administration of the new mine with Gundren
awards the party a 10 percent share of the mine's profits for their service
their deeds will be long remembered in this corner of the Sword Coast
Young Green Dragon is also known as Venomfang
